turns.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><defs><linearGradient id="skyGrad" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="0%" y2="100%"><stop offset="0%" style="stop-color:#87CEEB;stop-opacity:1" /><stop offset="100%" style="stop-color:#E0F6FF;stop-opacity:1" /></linearGradient></defs><rect width="512" height="512" fill="url(#skyGrad)"/><rect y="350" width="512" height="162" fill="#8B7355"/><g id="tree1"><rect x="80" y="280" width="20" height="100" fill="#654321"/><polygon points="90,200 40,280 140,280" fill="#228B22"/><polygon points="90,240 50,300 130,300" fill="#2E8B57"/><polygon points="90,270 60,320 120,320" fill="#228B22"/></g><g id="tree2"><rect x="200" y="260" width="24" height="120" fill="#654321"/><polygon points="212,160 150,260 274,260" fill="#2E8B57"/><polygon points="212,220 160,300 264,300" fill="#228B22"/><polygon points="212,270 170,340 254,340" fill="#2E8B57"/></g><g id="tree3"><rect x="340" y="290" width="18" height="90" fill="#654321"/><polygon points="349,210 305,290 393,290" fill="#228B22"/><polygon points="349,250 315,310 383,310" fill="#2E8B57"/><polygon points="349,280 325,330 373,330" fill="#228B22"/></g><g id="tree4"><rect x="420" y="270" width="22" height="110" fill="#654321"/><polygon points="431,170 375,270 487,270" fill="#2E8B57"/><polygon points="431,230 385,310 477,310" fill="#228B22"/><polygon points="431,280 395,350 467,350" fill="#2E8B57"/></g><g id="tree5"><rect x="140" y="300" width="16" height="80" fill="#654321"/><polygon points="148,230 110,300 186,300" fill="#228B22"/><polygon points="148,270 120,330 176,330" fill="#2E8B57"/></g><g id="tree6"><rect x="280" y="310" width="20" height="70" fill="#654321"/><polygon points="290,240 250,310 330,310" fill="#2E8B57"/><polygon points="290,280 260,340 320,340" fill="#228B22"/></g></svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}
